scenario_scope: Coordinated intake of fifteen primary optimizer sources spanning adaptive moments, weight decay, state-efficient and layer-scaled methods, matrix and curvature preconditioning, sign updates, scheduling, scale transfer, and Muon.

cases.decision_rationale: The existing chapter already owns optimizer, scheduler, numerical policy, state, topology, checkpointing, and qualification. The new literature supplies missing mechanism and selection depth without a distinct owner boundary.
non_claims: does not establish a universal optimizer winner
does not promote the governed-training core claim

cases.decision_rationale: A separate optimizer owner would split one executable run policy across chapters and duplicate full-state, schedule, topology, and qualification semantics.
required_before_drafting: not applicable; use the existing owner
evidence_transition_preconditions: a future observed distinct interface would require fresh adjudication
promotion_blockers: topic breadth alone is not a chapter boundary
non_claims: does not reserve a future optimizer chapter

validation_boundary: Records an existing-chapter source integration and rejects a duplicate chapter. It does not validate any optimizer implementation, theorem outside its assumptions, benchmark result, transfer claim, or training policy.
non_claims: does not authorize a model-training campaign or release
